title: Secure Bookstore API
statement:
Implement a secure bookstore API with authorization and role-based access control. The API should have the following endpoints:

**Protected Endpoints**

*   `GET /books`: Returns a list of all books in the store.
*   `GET /books/{bookId}`: Returns a specific book by its ID.
*   `POST /books`: Creates a new book in the store.
*   `PUT /books/{bookId}`: Updates an existing book in the store.
*   `DELETE /books/{bookId}`: Deletes a book from the store.

**Login Endpoint**

*   `POST /login`: Returns a JWT token upon successful login.

**Authorization**

*   The API should have two roles: `admin` and `reader`.
*   The `admin` role should have full access to all endpoints.
*   The `reader` role should only be able to read books (GET /books and GET /books/{bookId}).

**Implement the following features**

*   JWT token validation using the `jsonwebtoken` library.
*   Middleware to check the JWT token and authorize the user based on their role.
*   Error handling and logging.

**Constraints**

*   The API should use the `jsonwebtoken` library for JWT token validation.
*   The API should use a mock in-memory database for storing books.
*   The API should have a maximum of 10 concurrent requests.
*   The API should return a 401 Unauthorized response if the JWT token is invalid or missing.

**Visible Test Cases**

| Id | Method | Path | Body | Expected Status | Expected Body |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| v_tc1 | POST | /login | {"username": "admin", "password": "password"} | 200 | {"token": "..."} |
| v_tc2 | GET | /books |  | 200 | [{"id": 1, "title": "Book 1"}] |
| v_tc3 | GET | /books/1 |  | 200 | {"id": 1, "title": "Book 1"} |
| v_tc4 | POST | /books | {"title": "New Book"} | 201 | {"id": 2, "title": "New Book"} |
| v_tc5 | PUT | /books/1 | {"title": "Updated Book"} | 200 | {"id": 1, "title": "Updated Book"} |

**Hidden Test Cases**

| Id | Method | Path | Expected Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| h_tc1 | GET | /books | 401 |
| h_tc2 | GET | /books/1 | 401 |
| h_tc3 | POST | /books | 401 |
| h_tc4 | PUT | /books/1 | 401 |
| h_tc5 | DELETE | /books/1 | 401 |
constraints: Must use only the in-memory array or mock database provided
The API should use the jsonwebtoken library for JWT token validation

endpoints.description: Returns a list of all books in the store
Returns a specific book by its ID
Creates a new book in the store
Updates an existing book in the store
Deletes a book from the store
Returns a JWT token upon successful login
